description_long: This object is a traditional Japanese torii gate, commonly found at the entrance to Shinto shrines. It features two robust vertical pillars supporting two horizontal crossbars, with the upper crossbar elegantly curved upward. The structure is predominantly painted in a vivid red with black accents, and four decorative lanterns hang from the lower crossbar, adding to its ceremonial appearance. The wood grain texture is visible on the surfaces, suggesting a construction of lacquered timber. The torii's design is both symbolic and functional, marking the transition from the mundane to the sacred. Its imposing size and ornate details make it a prominent historical attraction in urban parks or shrine precincts.
description: A large, red and black traditional Japanese torii gate with curved crossbars and hanging lanterns, featuring wood grain texture and ornate details.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the torii gate, both vertical pillars, curved crossbars, and all four hanging lanterns clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the structure, the thickness of the crossbars, and the staggered placement of the pillars and lanterns.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with the same curved crossbars and lanterns, but the wood grain and pillar details are more pronounced.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the gate's depth and the alignment of the pillars and lanterns from the other side.
